observed_runtime_diff.dependency_note: No direct dependency was found between the launch dispatch timing change and the promote/stage-advance verification predicate change. They only share the runtime.py file and the broader CLI/runtime test suite.

buckets.change_summary: Adds a bounded settle loop after detached process spawn so short-lived probe scripts may materialize side effects before the command returns.
observed_hunk_shape: polls process.poll() until process exits or the deadline passes
canonical_risk: Launch success must remain bounded to explicit command packet truth.
Timing behavior must not become hidden runtime health truth.
Detached launch semantics must not silently become blocking runtime ownership.
affected_callers_or_surfaces: UI callers only as consumers of JSON command packets
existing_test_coverage_candidates: tests/test_cli.py launch-smoke stability and materialization cases
tests/test_cli.py exec-format and nonzero-launcher JSON packet cases
missing_later_contour_tests: prove the settle window is needed without relying on post-return polling
prove the wait remains bounded and cannot hang the command surface
prove return packet shape and command meaning are unchanged
prove launch smoke and launch client surfaces do not claim runtime health from the timing wait

independent_revertability.reason: Removing the settle loop restores prior detached spawn timing and does not reference promote, rollout, lock_acquired, selected backend, or policy-step state.

buckets.changed_surface_family: account promotion and rollout stage advance
buckets.change_summary: For lock_acquired=True, selected-backend participation is no longer required inside the local run_promote predicate; the owner path is expected to validate that requirement through postflight checks.
observed_hunk_shape: sets selected_backend_requirement_met to true when lock_acquired is true
keeps direct non-lock promote tied to selected_backend_verified
replaces backend_id in verified_selected_backend_ids in the promotion-success predicate
canonical_risk: Direct account promote must not inherit the stage-advance relaxation.
Stage advance must not claim success unless postflight proof owns the delegated selected-backend check.
Runtime truth must remain in strict JSON command packets, not inferred from caller narrative.
affected_callers_or_surfaces: UI callers only as consumers of JSON command packets
missing_later_contour_tests: prove direct run_promote without lock_acquired still requires selected-backend verification
prove lock-acquired stage-advance path delegates selected-backend proof to postflight verification
prove missing stage-advance postflight selected-backend proof fails closed
prove rollback behavior remains intact when active placement or policy proof fails

independent_revertability.reason: Reverting the selected_backend_requirement_met predicate logic does not require or affect the launch dispatch settle loop.

owner_options: split direct-promote and stage-advance proof rules further before implementation

ui_boundary.reason: The runtime.py tail remains dirty and owner approval is still required before UI freeze work can rely on or clean up around it.

independent_inspection.summary: Inspector confirmed two independent behavior buckets, identified launch dispatch impact on launch smoke and launch client surfaces, and listed stop conditions for any later execution contour.

stop_conditions_for_later_execution: either bucket starts affecting an unrelated command surface
run_promote with lock_acquired=true is discovered outside the stage-advance owner path
reverting one bucket changes the other bucket's command-surface tests
later tests require live runtime mutation without explicit owner authorization
UI work attempts to accept or hide the runtime tail
verification_required_before_accepting_a: targeted unit or CLI tests for the exact behavior
owner approval for accept or revert

decision_options: approve reverting one or both buckets
keep both pending and pause UI freeze work
